At the end of the day, YOU are the one sitting for the NCLEX. You are the one putting in the hours, showing up to study, pushing through the doubt, and fighting for your future.
The way you talk to yourself matters. Start speaking to yourself like someone who is going to pass. ✨

Summer in nursing school doesn’t really feel like a break… it just feels like a different kind of busy. ☀️📚

Whether you’re working shifts, studying for the NCLEX, or “resting” but mentally stuck thinking about exams, grades, and next semester—you’re not alone.

You’re still in

Outside of nursing, Kash spends time as Spider-Man, doing flips, acrobatics, and bringing smiles to children going through some of the hardest moments of their lives.

Because sometimes healing isn’t just medications and treatments… sometimes it’s laughter, hope, and making a chi

NDRIs: different from SSRIs & SNRIs 💊🧠
NDRI = Norepinephrine + Dopamine Reuptake Inhibitor
(Think bupropion.)
Remember:
✔️ Used for depression & smoking cessation
✔️ NO sexual dysfunction (common test point 👀)
✔️ Can increase risk of seizures
✔️ Avoid in patients with eating d

What is a select N question on the NCLEX? These are similar to select all that apply, but the # of options will be specified for you.
To practice NCLEX questions, try out our NCLEX q bank for free! Click the link in our bio to sign up 🙌

Nurse Routes to Take a Temperature (Most Common):

-Oral (mouth) = most common route; place under tongue (posterior sublingual pocket)

-Tympanic (ear) = place in ear per device instructions using provided cap/sheath

-Axillary (in armpit) = often 0.5 to 1°F LOWER than oral route

Types of needles you need to know before clinicals:

💉Blunt filter needle: draws up meds from glass ampules + filters out glass particles (never used for injection)
💉Blunt fill needle: draws from vials only, never used for injection
💉Injection needle: IM and SubQ injections

Choosing unstable before stable is a core NCLEX® prioritization strategy.
Stable clients have unchanged conditions with expected findings and consistent trends in vital signs and labs.
Unstable clients show change, new or unexpected findings, abnormal critical labs, or worsening

🚨 Adrenal Crisis Nursing Priorities
✅ Rapidly recognize the signs
✅ Replace volume → get that BP up
✅ Correct sodium & glucose
✅ Give stress-dose IV hydrocortisone
Adrenal crisis happens when the body doesn’t have enough cortisol to respond to stress. Without prompt treatment, p

Treatment for glomerulonephritis will depend on the cause of the inflammation. 🧐
Most commonly, this is a strep infection - so as nurses, we need to be educating our clients about the importance of completing the entire course of antibiotics! 💊
We also want to monitor I/Os 💧 f

🚗 Red blood cells are the Uber drivers of your body.
Their job? Deliver oxygen to every tissue and organ that needs it.
When anemia hits, those drivers start disappearing—and oxygen delivery slows down. The result? Fatigue, weakness, shortness of breath, and tissues that aren’t

📚 Placenta Previa vs. Abruption
🔹 Previa = Location problem
Placenta covers the cervix → bright red, painless bleeding
🔹 Abruption = Attachment problem
Placenta detaches → dark red bleeding, tender uterus, fetal distress
Remember:
Previa = Wrong location
Abruption = Wrong sepa

Sometimes your path into nursing starts with something personal.
